center.name: 新幾內亞高地
center.summary: 新幾內亞高地是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联31种鸟类，包括Chestnut forest rail、Archbold's nightjar、Modest tiger parrot等31种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Archbold's nightjar is a species endemic to New Guinea, dwelling in the highlands of the Central Cordillera mountain ranges. It has a patchy distribution, restricted to montane forests situated near clearings and open heathy regions. In fact, Archbold's nightjar is the sole high-mountain nightjar species of New Guinea, found in elevations of up to . Records indicate that this species is sedentary , exhibiting limited seasonal movement

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: First described by Christian Erich Hermann von Meyer in 1875, it is monotypic within the genus Oreocharis. It is found in the New Guinea Highlands and more scarcely in northern parts of the island, in subtropical or tropical moist montane forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The yellow-breasted satinbird is endemic to the central mountain ranges of New Guinea, where it occurs patchily from the Weyland Mountains eastward through the central cordillera to the Herzog Range and Wau Valley regions of Papua New Guinea. It inhabits lower montane and mid-montane forest, usually between 625 and 2,000 m elevation, though most records are from above 1,200 m. The species appears to prefer the interior of mature forest rather than forest edges, differing from related satinbirds that are more commonly associated with edge habitats. Although traditionally regarded as rare and poorly known, field surveys have found it locally common in some areas, particularly in montane primary forest
